His videos helped to explain some of the Engineering questions myself and no doubt many others have struggled to understand over the years, in a way and at a pace which made you feel appreciated and understood and I for one, hope his videos live on to continue to help and inspire another generation of creators. Thank you Robert. You will be very much missed.

author: @woodworks2123
text: Whenever anyone asks who inspired me to leave my career and pursue my engineering passion I always say Robert Murray smith. people in the field are like who's that. After I explain and ask them to watch his videos they understand. He's been a huge inspiration to me. His methodical approach and different approach to solving problems and looking at them differently have been massive influence. If I'm ever lucky enough to invent something significant and are asked I will give them Roberts name. A true forward thinker who deserves a huge amount of respect and adoration for what he has left us with. Please never take these videos down. They are a huge wealth of knowledge. I will hold him in my heart and thoughts throughout my life. Many thanks for sharing this sad news. I hope your family can find solace in this sad time.

author: @arcanondrum6543
text:
I was raised Roman Catholic.  I will definitely pray for Robert's Soul and I hope that he finds Peace.

Most certainly, there was a melancholy in Robert's voice of his last video.  I didn't want to mention it lest he be the reader of my comment.  I don't receive notification of replies anymore.  What if Robert had responded after I stopped checking or I forgot to check.

Robert's videos were immeasurably helpful in a world motivated by greed where "the Great Oz" wants to deliver Electricity to a helpless consumer.  As I hinted in a previous comment for a video long ago, I'm not yet able to fully take advantage of the knowledge that Robert shared but I am intelligent and hope that better days continue to draw closer and I can contribute as much or more but my clock is old and much of my time and definitely my money was taken.  Robert knew that the world could be cruel but he is gone too soon.

My condolences to your family and Robert's family.  I WILL Pray for Robert and for all who loved him.  I dreamt a simple event in my life, before it happened, 3 times.  Many people don't believe but I will pray for him.

author: @Dr.Fluffles
text: Current theories and evidence on the origins of consciousness lean more and more towards the idea that it stems from the broader entanglements and superpositions of quantum mechanics, linking the systems of our brains into a coherent form, but also extending beyond. That implies that each of our true "self"s exists in some form well beyond the extent of our bodies, in the connections we have made to the universe around us, to put it poetically. In this sense, if the science continues to move in the direction it seems, then no matter where one stands ideologically, it can be stated that some part of a person's mind, which in my opinion could be called the soul, continues to exist, indefinitely, in the entropy of the universe, in the ever growing entanglements we call the arrow of time. author: @watsoft70
text:
Thank you for making such a difficult announcement. So sorry for your family's loss Dave.

As a follower for a few years it was evident that the loss of his beloved Patti was too much to bear. I suspected that was the reason (or a good part of the reason) that he was ending his channel and asked him to look after his mental wellbeing in a comment on his final video...not knowing it was already too late.

I would never have expected that his exit would not be thoroughly researched. Unfortunately in some cases talking will never provide the comfort needed to overcome the grip of depression and while obviously sad at the family's and community's loss, I'm glad Robert is at peace and I'm glad he did it his way.

RIP Robert. Thank you for an engaging education, you will be missed.

author: @another3997
text: It's so hard to believe that Robert has gone. In his last video he seemed his usual, calm, self assured self.  But as someone who suffered a quite long and debilitating period of deep depression, I can perhaps understand a small part of his inner turmoil. To anyone out there that feels trapped in a situation, feels helpless or is bottling up their problems, don't be ashamed to talk to someone about it. It's not a sign of weakness, it doesn't make you a lesser person. It's a good thing.  Whether it be family, friends, a counsellor or a mental health charity, in person or on the phone... it DOES help. My condolences to Rob's family, thank you for making the hard decision to tell us about it. And of course, RIP Robert, thanks for all your interesting videos and making the world that bit better.  😢

author: @kevbrown2532
text:
Sorry to hear of your loss, of Roberts passing.
Having struggled with my own mental health for decades and frequently reached the point of wanting to end the misery of not having loved ones, in my case my daughters around me, I can well understand Roberts state of mind and possibly why he chose the actions he chose.

One thing I do need to point out and had I realised the  situation I'd have said this to Robert, his videos, his ingenuity, his infectious enthusiasm for creating things from what seemed to be scrap encouraged me to have a go. I'm not technically gifted as Robert was but he gave me the confidence to not expect perfection, to try things out, to experiment. All these have helped control my mental health issues, I now do see my daughters, life has improved. I have no doubt that Roberts attitude, videos and his personality made a huge difference to my state of mind.

Thank you for letting us know of this terrible news. I can only wish you, your family and all that knew Robert all the best for the future and my deepest condolences.

author: @Vercangetorix
text: As someone who’s tried to commit suicide and only ended up more disabled each time, I have a great deal of respect for him. I think it’s unfair for us to expect anybody to continue living behind a pay wall while falsely claiming such a police: society. The very structure of society under capitalism where we have a handful of owners and the rest of us are serfs is no different from feudalism. I’m always more in shock by the people who don’t see the disorder and want to continue that status quo while understanding those who choose to leave much more. We think attachment is love and so we allow ourselves to ignore our complicity in war, starvation, theft, and genocide. I just discovered him last week, with the farewell, and now I’m that much more committed to exploring his content. Much love, Mr. Murray ❤

text: I came to this channel just in the past year.  I always had an interest in tinkering and even went to engineering school when younger but quickly realized that I didn't have what it took.  His channel and his enthusiasm re-ignited that interest. He encouraged me and I'm sure the rest of the viewers to tackle our ideas and experiment.  This helped me give permission to myself to do "play" again in my retirement.  So, I am writing this because I want you, his family, to know that he made a difference and I am saddened by his loss.  And so from here in rural America, I send you my very sincere condolences.

replies.author: @Prof.Harry-Baulsach
replies.text:
I hear you. It's hard to lose his cheery explanations and simple yet effective creations. Even harder to reconcile the brilliant man we all watched with the news of his passing in misery. It's perhaps a demonstration of the old saying that behind every great man there's a great woman. And also a demonstration that some people are actually that much in love that they can't bear life apart. 
I'll miss him too, and will continue to build useful stuff using his ideas - and that's the best way to remember him, I guess. Tinkering to the benefit of others.

replies.author: @Psittacus_erithacus
replies.text:
It's interesting to me how my memory of certain internet teachers differs from that of IRL teachers I've had. I certainly treasure both, but we invite creator's videos in to our private time & spaces. Watch them on days when we wouldn't consider going out. I don't remember a specific instance, but I'm sure Robert did help me through a few rough patches over the years. Filling otherwise challenging time with gentle, interesting content. Even kindling interest in some project the pursuit of which would help lift me out of a bad place.

All just to say that what you're communicating makes perfect sense to me. Good on you too for expressing it openly. You're ahead of the curve there. Probably ahead of quite a few of us.
